consensus_trades.catalyst: CPI data release April 10, 2026; Cleveland Fed nowcast 3.16% suggests market overpriced on above-3.1% threshold
consensus_trades.risk_notes: Iran war oil shock could push inflation higher than forecast. Claude targets 3.1% threshold (NO at 0.95 price); Gemini targets 3.3% threshold (YES at 0.52). Both expect sub-consensus inflation on primary thresholds. Current price 0.95 (Claude trade) exceeds max_acceptable_price 0.28—requires vigilant entry discipline.

single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini did not analyze Fed rate cut contracts; Claude identified hawkish pivot driven by Iran war inflation and CME FedWatch data.
Gemini focused on near-term CPI data releases; Claude conducted longer-horizon recession analysis citing strong job growth and economist consensus at 30% recession probability.

contradictions.explanation: Claude bets NO on CPI > 3.1% (at 0.95 price, edge 20%); Gemini bets YES on CPI > 3.3% (at 0.52 price, edge 12%). Both forecast sub-consensus inflation but target different thresholds. Ranges overlap [0.68, 0.7]. This is a manifestation of threshold disagreement, not direction opposition on a single contract. Treated as consensus on inflation magnitude but with different strike selection. Gemini's 3.3% threshold offers less margin of safety than Claude's 3.1% given Cleveland Fed nowcast of 3.16%.

portfolio_concentration_warning: Inflation cluster (weight 5) at cluster cap. Fed policy (weight 4) and growth (weight 2) provide diversification. Claude's weather trade (NYC temp) was excluded from consensus and single-source—too low edge (8%) vs. weather threshold (5%), and resolution imminent (2026-04-07) with limited alpha window. Recommend deferring to next scan. Total portfolio relative weight: 11 across 3 uncorrelated clusters. Monitor for stagflation tail risk (inflation + recession simultaneously); current portfolio is hedged (long no-cut, long no-recession, short high-inflation).
reeval_triggers: CPI data release April 10, 2026 at 08:30 ET—immediate re-evaluation if print deviates >0.2% from Cleveland Fed nowcast
Fed speaker commentary on rate path in response to March inflation data
Geopolitical escalation in Iran conflict affecting oil prices >$90/bbl
Unexpected labor market weakness (jobs report <100k or unemployment tick >4.5%)
Market repricing of Fed cut expectations in CME FedWatch post-CPI
